title: STANDARD FOR COCOA (CACAO) MASS (COCOA/CHOCOLATE LIQUOR) AND COCOA CAKE CXS 141-1983

sections.section: STANDARD FOR COCOA (CACAO) MASS (COCOA/CHOCOLATE LIQUOR) AND COCOA CAKE CXS 141-1983
sections.summary: Title and revision history for the Codex Standard for Cocoa (Cacao) Mass (Cocoa/Chocolate Liquor) and Cocoa Cake (CXS 141-1983), adopted 1983 and revised/amended in 2001, 2014, 2016 and 2022.

Amends the prior text on labelling of non-retail containers by replacing specific container labelling provisions with a requirement that labelling of non-retail containers comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Applies the standard to Cocoa (Cacao) Mass (Cocoa/Chocolate Liquor) and Cocoa Cake for use in manufacturing cocoa and chocolate products, noting these products may also be sold directly to consumers.

Defines Cocoa Mass (product from cleaned cocoa nibs, with or without roasting and with/without removal or addition of constituents) and Cocoa Cake (product obtained by partial or complete removal of fat from cocoa nib or cocoa mass).

Defines Cocoa (Cacao) Mass (Cocoa/Chocolate Liquor) as the product obtained from cocoa nibs derived from merchantable-quality cocoa beans that have been cleaned and freed from shells as thoroughly as technically possible, and may be produced with or without roasting and with or without removal or addition of constituents.
Defines Cocoa Cake as the product obtained by partial or complete removal of fat from cocoa nib or cocoa mass.
Heading only for the quality/composition provisions applicable to Cocoa Mass (Cocoa/Chocolate Liquor); no substantive requirements in this section.

Specifies compositional limits for Cocoa Cake including cocoa shell and germ not more than 5% m/m on a fat‑free dry matter basis, cocoa butter content 47–60% m/m, and alternative alkali‑free‑basis limits for cocoa shell provided in the section.

Permits acidity regulators and emulsifiers in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 (and certain Table 3 entries) of the General Standard for Food Additives for food category 05.1.1 (cocoa mixes, cocoa mass/cake), and requires flavourings to comply with the Guidelines for the Use of Flavourings with only those not imitating chocolate or milk allowed at Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP).

Authorizes acidity regulators and emulsifiers for products covered by this Standard only as listed in Tables 1 and 2 (and certain Table 3 entries) of the General Standard for Food Additives for category 05.1.1 (cocoa mixes and cocoa mass/cake).
Requires flavourings used in products covered by this Standard to comply with CXG 66-2008 and permits only flavourings that do not imitate chocolate or milk, applied at GMP.

Recommends preparing and handling products in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene and relevant Codex texts, and requires compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

States additional labelling rules: prescribed product names for Cocoa Mass and Cocoa Cake and that names for internationally traded products must be acceptable to importing authorities, and requires labelling of non‑retail containers to follow CXS 346-2021.

Lists acceptable product names for the item defined in Section 2.1 (e.g., 'Cocoa (Cacao) Mass', 'Cocoa/Chocolate Liquor', 'Unsweetened Chocolate') and requires the name to be acceptable to importing authorities; designates the name 'Cocoa Cake' for the product in Section 2.2.
Requires labelling of non‑retail containers to conform to the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Specifies methods of analysis: determination of cocoa shell per AOAC 968.10 and 970.23, fat content per AOAC 963.15 or IOCCC 14 (1972), and lead per AOAC 934.07.
Prescribes determination of cocoa shell by AOAC methods 968.10 and 970.23.
Specifies determination of fat content according to AOAC 963.15 or IOCCC 14 (1972).
Specifies determination of lead according to AOAC 934.07.
